Any residents of the house­ hold (includ ing famil y member s, se rvants, and slaves, but not guests) receive a +3 bonus to sav­ ing throws again st the ev il eyc, as long as the scorpion ward remains in the doorway. [PDF_PAGE 10 | score=2] Definition: This spell creates a wall of swirling, opaque sand I-foot thick per level ofthc caster, 12 feet high, and 4 fcctlon g pcr lcvel ofthc caster. It must be cast so that it rests on a solid surface and must remain in contact with that surface, although the caster may move it about. The sand is thick and choking; insects cannot fly through iI, but are bat­ tered to death, their wings crus hed. All creatures save thosc with infravision will bc rendercd blind for as long as they are within Ihe wall's confines, and are - 2 on attack rolls and - 3 on AC on the round after they emerge from the wall, as they try 10 draw breath, clear their eyes, ears, mouths. and noses of sand, and so on. [PDF_PAGE 49 | score=2] ,Slaff ofSpell Focusing completely healed when it returns to staff form. If killed in snake form, it cannot return to staff form and it loses all magical properties. This item does not have or use charges. At the OM's optio n, the staff ca n be give n char ges. The use r can spe nd charges to add bonuses to Ihc snake's attack roll (+ 1 bonus per charge spent); up to five charges can be used in a single attack (for a +5 bonus). A cha rge can also be used to cu re the snake while it is in combat. The user casts a curing spell of any type and expends one charge to transfer the cure to the sna ke. The amount of curing is determined normally: no range limit applies. of Spell Focusing XP Va lue: 6
[PDF_PAGE 71 | score=2] ,Stone. Wardstone cannot detect zombies, skeletons, shadows, or similar undead. (Lenient OMs may allow the detection of other non cor poreal undead, suc h as shad­ ows, but should stric tly define which undead fall into this category.) 97-00 Choose one of the above powers. • Details for determining starting Strengths for wild powers are given in the CPU. page 20. Wild talents gain 4 PSPs for every level of experience they allain aller they acquire psionic pov.·ers. not for levels they possessed before such powers were gained. Reptili an Legionnaire XPValue: 1,400 GPVa lue: 7,000 DUNGEON M"STER Guide This sword gives an additional +3 bonus against such crea tures as dinosau rs, dragons , hydras, lizards, snakes, wyvems, and lizard men. Retaliator XP Value: 6,000 GP Value: 30,000 Legends & Lore Manannan macU r's avatar wields a sword called Retaliator that automatically slays his worst ene­ mies, the fire giants, when he stri kes (no saving throw). He can also call upon up to 100 HD wonh of undersea creatures to fight with him. The crea­ tures must be able to get to the scene of the battle under their own power, but once there, they fol­ 
[PDF_PAGE 282 | score=2] \ 08 09 10 II 12 \3 14 15 16 17 18 19 20 fema le, Charisma is lowered by 2 until she shaves. New, brown leather boots appear on the character's feet. Three gold pieces pop out of the well. A pou ch fi lled wi th 14 gold pieces drops on the character's head (1 point of damage). A purse filled with 30 platinum piece s appears at his or her feet. A loud burp comes from the well. The characte r's coin flies out of the well, with a bite out of il. Two*fool- Iong bea rd grows from the chin. The beard is always 2 feet long; it does not grow. If the character already had a bea rd, the bea rd is now 2 feel longer . If the characte r is female, Cha­ risma is lowered by 2 until she shaves. The hand le 
[PDF_PAGE 284 | score=2] , ing 1d4 points of damage per 10 feellravelcd, and remains there until the whee l is commanded to cease functioning. The user then falls from the ceiling and again takes damage unless protected by other means. The wheel can be activated three limes a day. of Fire XP Value: 1,800 GP Value: 9,000 Oriental Adventures These magical items are always found in pairs and look like large, ornately decorated chariot wheels. When mounted on a chariot and the comma nd v.urd spoke n, they burst into magical flame. The flame doe s not hinder the movement of the chariot, nor does it harm the vehicle, animal s, or occu­ pants. However, all creatures within 30 feet of the chariot suITer 2d6 points of damage
